events.text: Patient is a 49-year-old Hispanic male with no prior skin cancer history, presenting with a rapidly enlarging dark lesion on his left upper arm.

At age 49, patient was diagnosed with malignant melanoma (ICD-O morphology 8720/3) of the skin of the upper limb (ICD-O topography C44.6), staged as Stage IV (T3bN2cM1a) with in-transit metastases and distant subcutaneous metastases.

At age 49, excisional biopsy of the left upper arm revealed malignant melanoma with Breslow thickness of 3.5 mm, Clark level IV, ulceration present, mitotic rate 7/mm2, satellite lesions identified within 2 cm of the primary tumor, lymphovascular invasion present.
At age 49, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the left upper arm melanoma was performed with primary closure.
At age 49, separately excised in-transit satellite lesion from the left upper arm confirmed metastatic melanoma consistent with the primary tumor morphology.
At age 49, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the left axilla revealed 2 of 3 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma with largest deposit measuring 4.8 mm.
At age 49, sentinel lymph node pathology showed melanoma metastases in 2 of 3 nodes with no extracapsular extension, confirmed positive for S100, HMB-45, and SOX10.
At age 49, staging PET/CT showed FDG-avid left axillary lymphadenopathy, multiple subcutaneous nodules in the left arm and chest wall (SUVmax up to 5.2), and no visceral metastatic disease.
At age 49, MRI brain with gadolinium was negative for intracranial metastases.

At age 49, next-generation sequencing identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 40%), NRAS wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C250T), APC mutation, and tumor mutational burden of 11 mutations per megabase with microsatellite stable status.
At age 49, serum LDH was mildly elevated at 245 U/L, consistent with Stage IV M1a disease (subcutaneous distant metastases) with normal organ function.

At age 49, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks was initiated for 4 induction cycles for metastatic BRAF V600E melanoma, with immunotherapy chosen first given high TMB.

At age 49, after cycle 3 of ipilimumab/nivolumab, patient developed grade 3 immune-related hepatitis with ALT 425 U/L and AST 380 U/L, requiring ipilimumab discontinuation and high-dose IV methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg.
At age 49, hepatitis improved to grade 1 after 2 weeks of high-dose corticosteroids followed by prolonged oral prednisone taper over 8 weeks.
At age 49, nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks was continued as maintenance after ipilimumab was permanently discontinued due to hepatotoxicity.
At age 49, restaging CT at 12 weeks showed partial response with resolution of subcutaneous nodules and decreased axillary lymphadenopathy.
At age 50, 6-month restaging PET/CT showed near complete metabolic response with minimal residual FDG activity in the left axilla.
At age 50, LDH normalized to 188 U/L and patient continued nivolumab maintenance with excellent response and ECOG performance status of 0.
At age 50, patient developed grade 1 vitiligo affecting bilateral hands and periocular regions, considered a favorable prognostic sign and requiring no treatment intervention.
At age 50, 12-month restaging CT showed complete radiographic response with no measurable disease per RECIST 1.1.
At age 50, after achieving complete response on nivolumab for 12 months, treatment was electively discontinued with plan for close surveillance imaging every 3 months.

scenario_blurb: Stage III/IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation. Treated with combination BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib/trametinib) and/or checkpoint immunotherapy (nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab). Some with brain metastases. Include LDH levels.
